conventions.rule: Component files use PascalCase matching the component name
Utility files use kebab-case with descriptive suffix
Tables camelCase in Drizzle, IDs are CUID2 text via createId()
Every protected resource has both UI-level and API-level guards
conventions.example: (admin) layout redirect + requireAdmin() in API routes

conventions.rule: Server Components for data fetching, Client Components for interactivity, API routes for mutations
Named exports only for components (no default exports)
API handlers follow: auth guard → input validation → DB operation → response
Auth guard uses discriminated union: if ('error' in auth) return auth.error
conventions.example: const auth = await requireAuth(request); if ('error' in auth) return auth.error;

conventions.rule: Drizzle numeric fields serialized via serializeDecimal() at route handler level
Tests colocated with source files using .test.ts/.test.tsx suffix
Canonical test pattern: vi.hoisted() → vi.mock() → import handler
Dark theme via CSS variables in globals.css, Tailwind v4 with @theme inline
No page entrance animations; animate-fade-up only for interactive elements
